Worn Shai`din Naginata  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Slashing Atk Delay: 45
DMG: 37 Dmg Bonus: 155 AC: 4
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +6 STA: +6 WIS: +2 INT: +2
WT: 5.5 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

weapon graphic is the same as https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/item.html?item=6817
▲ Page top
Naginata not bad
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2003 at 6:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788439512
11 posts
On Rodcet Nife got Naginata for 1000p was 1500p traded away FBSS was selling for 2000p. quite a steal, using it now hitting max for 130 at level 34 and 189 strength buffed. Good damage, on the slow side but have 30% haste. Good stats, wish it had a damage dealing proc. Will use it until I save up for a reaver. Then will keep this and use this to kill what the reaver can't touch. Worth the trade. Looks like an Asian weapon a samurai would use.

Hifglar Stormblade
34 Paladin
▲ Page top
Re: eh
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2002 at 4:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361764343
17 posts
Actually I've used both Black Acrylia Halbred and this weapon. I'll take the naganata over Halbred any day of the week. BAH looks pretty and the fx is nice, but I'll take ac/str/sta over a proc any day :) Anyways, does some nice dmg, hit for max 235 @ lvl 58(just dinged so not sure if 2hs maxed yet or not..) Also have CF1, best crit so far was 397. Any form of haste makes this little baby look pretty impressive.
Anyways, Grats to those who have acquired and GL to all the hopefulls =)
▲ Page top
Damage bonus
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2002 at 9:56 PM Rating: Decent
-REDACTED-
3 posts
Damage bonus are in fact level based and only melee classes aquire them, also the bonus applies only to the main hand weapon so hits below it are possible if you are duel wielding
▲ Page top
eh
QuoteReply
# Aug 16 2002 at 5:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759157062
11 posts
yeah this is good and all but i really dont like how it looks being held in the hand... it looks kinda gay. I mean, its not that stylish and it's... just... ick. So i got a Black acrylia halberd. dam40 del49 magicdmg4 effect lifedraw (45dmg lifetap). I accually like this more than the worn shin thing. yeah, it has nice str and what not, but i'd really like the higher dmg and effect.

(btw, i have to say that i beleive that dmg bonus is determined by Level and Delay. When you lvl, the bonus rises. However, the weapons with the higher delay get higher Dmg bonuses. (with my 45 pally, the dmg bonus of a 49delay weap is 15ish. The bonus of a 22 delay weap is 7ish. the bonus on a weighted axe (150 delay) is 45ish.)


Also, you know that 'shoot the DVD' game/ad that you sometimes see at the top of a page? I got high score of 602 DVD's shot =) =) =)
▲ Page top
RE: eh
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2003 at 2:53 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778987524
65 posts
Well I like the look - quite elegant looking compared to the big axe.

Anyway the damage bonus is the same for weapons with delay 40 and under. Weapons slower than 40 start getting progressively bigger bonuses as they get slower.

I met a uber twinked dwarf lvl6 i guess... and saved him from death, bandaged him and got him on his way. 10 min after he gave me a tell "Do you need a better weapom?" . The rest is history. My now 13th lvl warrior is now ubertwinked also with this awsome pigsiticka!! Just had to tell =)

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 12 2001 at 10:56 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I posted this weapon as a 17 SK and if they don't have a dmg bonus: 0, then the weapon does not have any kind of dmg bonus on it period, or the class in mention can't use the bonus, if the weapon does have the line of dmg bonus: 0, then that means the weapon is capable of a dmg bonus but cannot have a dmg bonus due to lvl, and thats how its works
RE: Hey
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 10:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757661257
5 posts
Damage bonues is based completely on level. You recive none untill after 20 (I belive 25). The Damage bonues is not part fo the weapon. Case inpoint, take that weapon from your level 17 SK and hand it to a guildmate SK of arround 40th level and the dmage bonues will read 15 or so when he inspects it. That informs the chracter how much extra damage he/she will have with that weapon not how much extra damage the weapon its self does.
▲ Page top
RE: Hey
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2002 at 9:54 AM Rating: Decent
Darknyss
Scholar
551 posts
Also, the Damage Bonus is not "extra" damage... it's the base damage that you will hit with... so a damage bonus of 15 means that they will never hit for less then 15 base damage... this becomes extremely important to a dual weilding class, as lower delay becomes more damage efficient with increasing damage bonus for higher levels.

Most melee classes will not get a bonus to higher delay weapons until the delay of the weapon is greater than 40... (pally/sk get bonus to 2H though)... so a 15 30 weapon would have the same dmg bonus as a 9 18... the difference? you'd be hitting for at LEAST the base damage bonus much more with the lower delay weapon.

of course, when high level haste, damage bonus, disciplines, ratios, spell effects, buffs/debuffs are taken into account... it really becomes an impossible task to determine the value of a weapon and it's damage efficiency...
____________________________
Pain Lord Darknyss Grimscale
92 Iksar Crusader of the Greenmist
▲ Page top
RE: Hey
QuoteReply
# Nov 21 2001 at 5:55 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361490966
View User Forum
32 posts
erm...ancalagon, go back and read the message you replied to. he said exactly the same thing you did. so um...thanks i guess for your wonderful insight.
▲ Page top
RE: Hey
QuoteReply
# Mar 10 2002 at 12:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769261394
View User Forum
34 posts
I understood Ancalagon, the previous post made little sense to me. Another factor that is important in determining damage bonus is the delay of the weapon; the higher the delay the greater the damage bonus when all other things are equal.

▲ Page top
Damage Bonuses
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 4:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758414225
View User Forum
46 posts
Damage Bonus's start appearing on weapons (Especially 2-Handed) from level 20 onwards.

My 20th Warrior had a Damage Bonus of 1 on his 40del 2HS at level 20. At 35 it's now 6
▲ Page top
RE: Damage Bonuses
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 12:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252000171
3 posts
Thanks for the tip Peterion. This would explain how the guy selling me a Blade of Xalgoz said it had a damage bonus of 3, and when I got it it didn't even have a damage bonus! I was confused hehe
▲ Page top
RE: Damage Bonuses
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 11:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246577615
65 posts
Now correct me if I'm wrong but I didn't notice damage bonuses until lvl 28.

Before all this patching when a warrior got a critical hit, you could tell the difference between your actual damage done, and the critical hit. This telling you there is a damage bonus. I can't remember clearly if this was the wurmslayer I was using or the smouldering brand. Because wurmslayer has a delay of 40 its going to have 1 more point of damage higher on its damage bonus then my smouldering brand.
▲ Page top
RE: Damage Bonuses
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 7:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1642983438479
Scholar
263 posts
Crits also reflect str bonus. Pre-Nov patch, the off hand crit damage was the same as the announced damage while the main hand (the one where str counts) had a higher damage done that the announced damage. The patch fixed this.

However, the above mentioned bonus is not the one listed on the item...that is the 2hand bonus and it varies based on level and delay of item.

just another DENecro

with a couple alts =)
▲ Page top
RE: Damage Bonuses
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 6:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361661281
View User Forum
3 posts
Damage bonuses are set based upon your character.
They change depending on your level and weapon skills.

Yeah, anything with the damage higher than the delay is considered godly. This is a ratio of less than 1, and there are very few weapons in the everquest world that have that kind of ratio.

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 14 2001 at 3:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) OMG!!! yer the only person who said it plain and simple.
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 09 2001 at 6:31 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) whats this dmg bonus thing? everyone says you have to be 50+ but i'm a 35sk with a sentry maul (2h blunt) and the dmg bonus on it for me currently is 8...soooo, whats the deal?
RE: eh
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 10:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248526746
29 posts
You have to be 50+ to get the newly added 2hander bonus. Old rules on dmg bonus (depending on char level, weapon´s delay, str and weapon skill level) still apply.
▲ Page top
No Damage bonus?
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 4:53 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361541673
6 posts
How strange... no damage bonus... I thought that 2hs weapons all had that. Anyone know why this one doesn't have it?

Claera - Zebuxoruk
▲ Page top
RE: No Damage bonus?
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 5:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361623581
View User Forum
9 posts
Damage bonus for two handed weapons only shows up if the person viewing the item can use the two-hander in question.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 09 2001 at 6:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Not true, my Rogue friend can parse all 2hs damage bonuses, and she can only use the elegant Darkwood Katana from General V'Ghera (At least that we have found so far).
RE: No Damage bonus?
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 4:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756515749
112 posts
The screenshot was probably taken by someone who either, wasn't 51+, or can't use 2hs or somesuch.

The damage bonus only shows up the same to people of the same level. Since damage bonus is so tied to the delay of the weapon, I'd wager the dmg bonus is 29 at lvl 60, as that is the dmg bonus on the SK epic, with delay of 45.

-bort
